_source: Allen et al. (1998) FAO-56 Ch 4; groundSpring Exp 003 error propagation; Gong et al. (2006) 'Sensitivity of PM ET₀'
_note: Benchmark for one-at-a-time (OAT) sensitivity analysis of FAO-56 Penman-Monteith ET₀. Quantifies which input variable dominates the ET₀ response.

_provenance.method: OAT sensitivity: perturb each input ±10% from baseline, compute ΔET₀/Δx. Baseline: FAO-56 Example 18 (Uccle, Belgium, July). Complements groundSpring Exp 003 Monte Carlo error propagation.
references: Gong et al. (2006) Ag Water Mgmt 86:57-63
Estévez et al. (2009) J Irrigation Drainage Eng 135(3):275-286
groundSpring Exp 003: humidity dominates at 66% of ET₀ variance
_provenance._tolerance_justification: Sensitivity index: 0.05 (numerical derivative stability). Rank ordering: deterministic for given baseline. Elasticity: 0.02 (ratio stability).

sensitivity_ranking.description: Variables ranked by absolute sensitivity index. Literature consensus: Rs and humidity dominate, wind is secondary, temperature contributes through VPD.
sensitivity_ranking.note: groundSpring Exp 003 found humidity at 66% of variance. Gong et al. (2006) found Rs > RH > wind > T for humid climates.
sensitivity_ranking.note2: At least one radiation or humidity variable must be in the top 2 by absolute sensitivity

elasticity_bounds.description: Elasticity (% change in ET₀ per % change in input) should be [-2, 2] for all variables

monotonicity.description: ET₀ must increase with more energy input (Rs, T) and decrease with more humidity (RH)

symmetry.description: Sensitivity should be approximately symmetric: |S(+10%)| ≈ |S(-10%)| within 50%

multi_site_consistency.description: Sensitivity ranking consistent across 3 climatic conditions
